blocks:
Sūrat al-Ṣaff

And it is named Sūrat al-Ḥawāriyyīn (The Chapter of the Disciples) and Sūrat ʿĪsā (The Chapter of Jesus). It is Medinan (Madaniyyah). And it is fourteen verses without dispute.* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ (1) Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٢) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِمَ تَقُولُونَ ما لا تَفْعَلُونَ ** (2) O you who have faith! Why do you say what you do not do? It is narrated that the Muslims said: "If we knew the dearest deeds to Allah, we would sacrifice our wealth and our souls for them." So Allah revealed: إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا ** (Indeed, Allah loves those who fight in His way in ranks). Then they turned away on the Day of Uhud, so [this verse] was revealed. And al-Qummi [said it is] addressing the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله who promised Him that they would support Him, not disobey His command, and not break their covenant regarding the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. So Allah knew that they would not fulfill what they said. And Allah named them the believers (المؤمنين) based on their affirmation (إقرارهم), even if they did not speak the truth (يصدّقوا).

(٣)** كَبُرَ مَقْتاً عِنْدَ اللهِ أَنْ تَقُولُوا ما لا تَفْعَلُونَ ** (3) It is greatly hateful to Allah that you should say what you do not do.

Al-Maqt (المقت) is the severest hatred. In Nahj al-Balaghah: Breaking a promise (الخلف) necessitates detestation (المقت) with Allah and with people. The Almighty Allah said: **كَبُرَ مَقْتاً عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (the Ayah).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The promise (عدة) of a believer to his brother is a vow (نذر) for which there is no expiation (كفّارة). So whoever breaks the promise, he has begun by breaking the oath of Allah (بحلف الله بدأ) and has exposed himself to His detestation (لمقته تعرّض). And that is His saying: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (the two Ayahs).

(٤) إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا ** (Indeed Allah loves those who fight in His way in ranks) standing in ranks (مصطفّين), as if they were بُنْيانٌ مَرْصُوصٌ (a compact structure), in their compactness without any gap (فرجة). And al-raṣṣ (الرّصّ) is the joining of some parts of the structure to others and its firmness (استحكامه).

In Miṣbāḥ al-Mutahajjid (مصباح المتهجّد), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a sermon which He delivered on the Day of al-Ghadīr, He said: And know, O believers, that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا ** (Indeed Allah loves those who fight in His way in ranks). Do you know what the way (سبيل) of Allah is, and who is His way (سبيله)? I am the way (سبيل) of Allah Whom He appointed me for obedience (للاتّباع) after His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(٥) وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِقَوْمِهِ يا قَوْمِ لِمَ تُؤْذُونَنِي وَقَدْ تَعْلَمُونَ أَنِّي رَسُولُ اللهِ إِلَيْكُمْ ** (5) And when Moses said to his people, 'O my people! Why do you torment me, though you know that I am Allah's apostle to you?' And the knowledge of the apostleship necessitates reverence (التعظيم) and prevents harm (الإيذاء) in the assembly (المجمع). It is narrated concerning the story of Qarun that he secretly sent a woman to him (Moses) and claimed that he (Moses) had committed adultery with her, and they accused him of killing Harun. فَلَمَّا زاغُوا ** So when they swerved from the truth (الحقّ), أَزاغَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ ** Allah made their hearts swerve He turned them away (صرفها) from accepting the truth (الحقّ) and inclining toward what is right (الصواب). Al-Qummi said: meaning, He caused doubt (شكك) in their hearts (قلوبهم). وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ.** And Allah does not guide the transgressing lot.

(٦)** وَإِذْ قالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللهِ إِلَيْكُمْ مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ مِنَ التَّوْراةِ وَمُبَشِّراً بِرَسُولٍ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِي اسْمُهُ أَحْمَدُ ** (6) And when Jesus son of Mary said, 'O Children of Israel! Indeed, I am the apostle of Allah to you, to confirm what is before me of the Torah, and to give the good news of an apostle who will come after me, whose name is Ahmad.'

meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. And the meaning is that my religion is the confirmation of the Books of Allah and His Prophets.

In al-Awali: In the tradition (الحديث): When Allah the Almighty gave Jesus عليه السلام the good news of the appearance of our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said to him concerning his description: "And take counsel regarding the owner of the red camel, the bright-faced (الأقمر) one, the husband of women (نكّاح النساء)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When Allah sent the Messiah عليه السلام, He said: "Indeed, a Prophet whose name is Ahmad صلى الله عليه وآله will come after me, from the progeny of Ishmael (إسماعيل). He will come confirming me and confirming you, and justifying me and justifying you."

And from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: The Prophets never ceased giving good news of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله until Allah sent the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary عليه السلام, and he gave good news of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: يَجِدُونَهُ (they find Him) — meaning the Jews and the Christians, meaning the description of Muhammad and his name — عِنْدَهُمْ (with them) — meaning — فِي التَّوْراةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ يَأْمُرُهُمْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهاهُمْ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ (in the Torah and the Evangel, bidding them to what is right and forbidding them from what is wrong). And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informing about Jesus عليه السلام: وَمُبَشِّراً بِرَسُولٍ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِي اسْمُهُ أَحْمَدُ. (and to give the good news of an apostle who will come after me, whose name is Ahmad.)

And in al-Faqih, narrated from him عليه السلام: Indeed, the name of the Prophet in the Scrolls of Abraham (upon our Prophet and his Progeny and upon him be peace) is al-Mahi (الماحي), and in the Torah of Moses is al-Hadd (الحادّ), and in the Evangel of Jesus عليه السلام is Ahmad (أحمد), and in the Criterion (الفرقان) is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله.

And al-Qummi (narrates): Some of the Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Why were you named Ahmad صلى الله عليه وآله?" He replied: "Because in the heaven I am more praised (أحمد) than I am on the earth."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: There were five hundred years between Jesus and Muhammad (upon him and his Progeny and upon him be peace), two hundred and fifty years of which had neither a Prophet nor a manifest scholar (عالم ظاهر). They were holding fast to the religion of Jesus عليه السلام. Then he said: "And the earth is never without a scholar (عالم)." فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ قالُوا هذا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ. (But when he brought them manifest proofs, they said, 'This is plain magic.')

(٧) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُوَ يُدْعى إِلَى الْإِسْلامِ ** (7) And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, while he is being invited to Islam? Meaning, no one is a greater wrongdoer than him who is invited to Islam—whose truth is manifest and which necessitates for him the good of both abodes (خير الدّارين)—yet he puts in place of his acceptance the fabrication of a lie against Allah by denying His Messenger and calling His signs (آياته) magic (سحراً). وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. He does not guide them to that which contains their success (فلاحهم).

(٨) يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطْفِؤُا نُورَ اللهِ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ ** (8) They desire to put out the light of Allah with their mouths His proof (حجّته) by their slandering Him/it (بطعنهم فيه) وَاللهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِ ** bringing it to its ultimate goal (مبلّغ غايته) by spreading it and proclaiming it. And it was read with the genitive annexation (بالإضافة). وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكافِرُونَ though the faithless should be averse, as an enforcement (ارغاماً) against them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطْفِؤُا ** the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) ** بِأَفْواهِهِمْ وَاللهُ مُتِمُ ** the Imamate (الإمامة), due to His saying: فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالنُّورِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْنا (So have faith in Allah and His Apostle and the Light which We have sent down). So the Light (النّور) is the Imam (الإِمام).

And al-Qummi (said regarding) وَاللهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِ : He said: (It refers) to the al-Qa'im (بالقائم) from the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), when he emerges, Allah will make him prevail over the entire religion (يظهره الله على الدين كلّه) until none is worshipped except Allah.

(٩) هُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالْهُدى وَدِينِ الْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ ** (9) It is He who has sent His Apostle with the guidance and the religion of truth, that He may make it prevail over all religions, so that He may make it prevail over all religions (ليغلبه على جميع الأديان) وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ ** though the polytheists should be averse. due to what it contains of pure monotheism (محض التوحيد) and the nullification of polytheism (إبطال الشرك). Its interpretation preceded in Surat al-Tawbah (سورة التوبة).

(١٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى تِجارَةٍ تُنْجِيكُمْ ** (10) O you who have faith! Shall I show you a commerce that will deliver you And it was read with tashdid (بالتشديد) مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ. from a painful punishment. (١١) تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ بِأَمْوالِكُمْ وَأَنْفُسِكُمْ ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ. (11) that you have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and wage jihad in the way of Allah with your possessions and your lives. That is better for you, should you know. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the first Ayah. So they said, "If we knew what it was, we would expend for it our possessions, our lives, and our children." Then Allah said: تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ ** (the two Ayahs).

(١٢) يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ وَمَساكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (12) He will forgive you your sins, and admit you into gardens with streams running in them, and goodly dwellings in the Gardens of Eden. That is the great success. (١٣) وَأُخْرى تُحِبُّونَها ** (13) And another [blessing] which you love: And for you, in addition to this mentioned blessing, is another beloved blessing. And in this is an allusion (تعريض) that they prefer the immediate (العاجل) over the deferred (الآجل). نَصْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ ** (a help from Allah and a victory near at hand.) Immediate. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, in this world, through the victory (فتح) of al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). And he also said: The conquest (فتح) of Mecca. وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (And give good news to the faithful.)

(١٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا أَنْصارَ اللهِ ** (14) O you who have faith! Be Allah’s helpers وقرئ بالتنوين واللّام And it was recited (وقرئ) with tanwin (التنوين) and the lam (اللام) كَما قالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوارِيِّينَ مَنْ أَنْصارِي إِلَى اللهِ ** just as Jesus son of Mary said to the Disciples, ‘Who are my helpers for Allah?’ meaning: Who is from my army (جندي) heading towards the help (نصرة) of Allah? And the Disciples (الحواريّين) are His chosen ones (أصفياؤه). The explanation (تفسير) of the Disciple (الحواريّ) has already preceded in Surat Āl ‘Imrān. قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنْصارُ اللهِ فَآمَنَتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ وَكَفَرَتْ طائِفَةٌ فَأَيَّدْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلى عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا ظاهِرِينَ ** The Disciples said, ‘We are Allah’s helpers.’ So a faction of the Children of Israel believed, and a faction disbelieved. Then We supported those who had believed against their enemy, and they became victors. so they became dominant (غالبين).

In Thawāb al-A‘māl (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma‘ (المجمع), narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Sūrat al-Ṣaff (سورة الصفّ) and persists in its recitation during his obligatory prayers (فرائضه) and supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will line him up (صفّه) with His angels and His Messengers, the Prophets (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all).
